work_packages.title: Project Management and Coordination
objectives: Ensure effective consortium governance, administrative management, and reporting throughout the project.
Maintain quality assurance, risk management, and ethics compliance across all project activities.

tasks.title: Consortium governance and financial management
Quality assurance and risk monitoring
Ethics compliance oversight and coordination with GenAI4EU Hub

work_packages.title: Neuro-Symbolic Planning and Reasoning Engine
objectives: Design and implement a hybrid neuro-symbolic planning engine with formal sub-task graph decomposition.
Integrate MCTS and HTN planning with LLM-based heuristics and verifiable plan self-correction.

tasks.title: Sub-task graph decomposition design with formal pre/post-conditions
MCTS and HTN planning integration with LLM heuristics
Plan monitoring and self-correction implementation

work_packages.title: Adaptive Memory Architecture
objectives: Design and implement a unified tri-store memory architecture with a learned memory controller.
Develop memory indexing, retrieval, and forgetting mechanisms for long-horizon agent interactions.

tasks.title: Tri-store memory architecture design
Memory indexing and retrieval strategy implementation
Forgetting mechanism and coherence maintenance

work_packages.title: Decentralised Multi-Agent Coordination
objectives: Develop formally specified task-delegation and negotiation protocols for heterogeneous AI agents.
Demonstrate convergence guarantees and auditable multi-agent interaction with shared tool access governance.

tasks.title: Task-delegation and negotiation protocol design
Convergence and conflict resolution under partial observability
Auditable multi-agent interaction trace module

work_packages.title: Healthcare Demonstrator — Clinical Decision Support
objectives: Validate the MAESTRO framework in a clinical decision support use case with HL7 FHIR integration.
Conduct clinical expert validation of specialist sub-agents against diagnostic benchmarks.

tasks.title: Clinical use case definition and diagnostic benchmark design
HL7 FHIR integration and specialist sub-agent deployment
Clinical expert validation and results analysis

deliverables.title: Clinical decision support use case specification

work_packages.title: Manufacturing Demonstrator — Process Optimisation
objectives: Validate the MAESTRO framework for manufacturing process optimisation in a pilot factory setting.
Demonstrate multi-agent real-time coordination across production segments against defined KPIs.

tasks.title: Production scenario definition and KPI framework design
Digital twin integration and multi-agent coordination deployment
Pilot factory operational validation

deliverables.title: Manufacturing use case and KPI specification

work_packages.title: Logistics Transfer Demonstrator — Cross-Sector Generalisation
objectives: Validate MAESTRO generalisation in logistics and supply chain operations across three international corridors.
Confirm integrated architecture transferability beyond primary call sectors using real operational data.

tasks.title: Logistics scenario and disruption recovery benchmark design
Agent-based planning and negotiation deployment across supply chain
Operational validation across three international corridors

deliverables.title: Logistics demonstrator specification and benchmark report

work_packages.title: Integration, Evaluation and Benchmarking
objectives: Define a cross-cutting evaluation framework and integrate all MAESTRO research pillars into a unified system.
Produce a standardised benchmark suite for community adoption and cross-demonstrator evaluation.

tasks.title: Cross-cutting evaluation framework and KPI definition
MAESTRO unified framework integration
Cross-demonstrator benchmarking and community benchmark release

work_packages.title: Dissemination, Exploitation and Communication
objectives: Manage open-source framework release, community building, and AI-on-demand platform contributions.
Develop and execute consortium exploitation strategy and lead public communication activities.

tasks.title: Open-source release management and community building
Exploitation strategy development and IP management
Communication, website, and public engagement

edges.derivation: WP5 objective 'Validate the MAESTRO framework' with 'specialist sub-agents' (T5-02: HL7 FHIR integration and specialist sub-agent deployment) requires the neuro-symbolic planning engine produced by WP2 (D2-02); identifiable from WP2 and WP5 objectives within wp_structure.json

WP5 specialist sub-agent operations in a clinical decision support setting require the adaptive memory system (WP3) for indexing and retrieval during long-horizon clinical interactions; WP3 objective 'for long-horizon agent interactions' maps to clinical sub-agent memory needs; identifiable from WP3 and WP5 objectives within wp_structure.json

T5-02 'specialist sub-agent deployment' requires coordination and task-delegation protocols from WP4 (D4-02: Decentralised coordination software); WP4 objective 'task-delegation and negotiation protocols for heterogeneous AI agents' is a prerequisite for multi-specialist-agent clinical deployment; identifiable from WP4 and WP5 task titles within wp_structure.json

WP6 objective 'Demonstrate multi-agent real-time coordination across production segments' relies on planning engine outputs from WP2; T6-02 'Digital twin integration and multi-agent coordination deployment' requires the plan monitoring and self-correction capability from T2-03; identifiable from WP2 and WP6 objectives within wp_structure.json

WP6 multi-agent manufacturing demonstrator agents require adaptive memory (WP3) to maintain state across production segments; WP3 tri-store memory architecture supports the agent interaction continuity needed for real-time manufacturing coordination; identifiable from WP3 and WP6 objectives within wp_structure.json

T6-02 'multi-agent coordination deployment' explicitly requires WP4 coordination protocols (D4-02); WP6 objective 'multi-agent real-time coordination' is a direct application of WP4 outputs; identifiable from WP4 and WP6 task titles within wp_structure.json

T7-02 'Agent-based planning and negotiation deployment across supply chain' explicitly requires the agent-based planning engine from WP2 (D2-02: Planning engine software prototype); identifiable from WP2 deliverable and WP7 T7-02 task title within wp_structure.json

WP7 objective 'Validate MAESTRO generalisation in logistics and supply chain operations across three international corridors' involves long-horizon multi-corridor agent operations requiring memory indexing and retrieval from WP3; WP3 objective 'for long-horizon agent interactions' directly maps to logistics corridor scenarios; identifiable from WP3 and WP7 objectives within wp_structure.json

T7-02 'Agent-based planning and negotiation deployment across supply chain' explicitly requires negotiation protocols from WP4 (D4-01: Multi-agent coordination protocol specification; D4-02: Decentralised coordination software); identifiable from WP4 deliverables and WP7 T7-02 task title within wp_structure.json

WP8 objective 'integrate all MAESTRO research pillars into a unified system'; T8-02 'MAESTRO unified framework integration' explicitly integrates WP2 as a named research pillar; D2-02 (Planning engine software prototype) is a direct input to T8-02; identifiable from WP8 objective and T8-02 task title within wp_structure.json

WP8 objective 'integrate all MAESTRO research pillars into a unified system'; T8-02 'MAESTRO unified framework integration' integrates WP3 (Adaptive Memory Architecture) as a named research pillar; D3-02 (Adaptive memory system software) is a direct input to T8-02; identifiable from WP8 objective and T8-02 task title within wp_structure.json

WP8 objective 'integrate all MAESTRO research pillars into a unified system'; T8-02 'MAESTRO unified framework integration' integrates WP4 (Decentralised Multi-Agent Coordination) as a named research pillar; D4-02 (Decentralised coordination software) is a direct input to T8-02; identifiable from WP8 objective and T8-02 task title within wp_structure.json

T8-03 'Cross-demonstrator benchmarking and community benchmark release' requires evaluation results from WP5 (D5-02: Healthcare demonstrator evaluation dataset); WP8 objective 'cross-demonstrator evaluation' cannot proceed without WP5 output; identifiable from WP8 T8-03 task title and WP5 D5-02 deliverable within wp_structure.json

T8-03 'Cross-demonstrator benchmarking and community benchmark release' requires evaluation results from WP6 (D6-02: Manufacturing process optimisation demonstrator software outputs); WP8 objective 'cross-demonstrator evaluation' cannot proceed without WP6 output; identifiable from WP8 T8-03 task title and WP6 D6-02 deliverable within wp_structure.json

T8-03 'Cross-demonstrator benchmarking and community benchmark release' requires evaluation results from WP7 (D7-02: Cross-sector generalisation evaluation dataset); WP8 objective 'cross-demonstrator evaluation' cannot proceed without WP7 output; identifiable from WP8 T8-03 task title and WP7 D7-02 deliverable within wp_structure.json

WP9 objective 'Manage open-source framework release' requires the unified integrated MAESTRO framework produced by WP8 (T8-02); T9-01 'Open-source release management' depends on D8-03 (Final integration and evaluation report) from WP8; D9-03 (Final dissemination and impact report) must reflect WP8 final evaluation findings; identifiable from WP8 and WP9 objectives and deliverables within wp_structure.json

critical_path_analysis.algorithm: Longest-path dynamic programming over topological sort (Kahn's algorithm)
critical_path_analysis.cycle_check_method: Kahn's topological sort — all 36 nodes processed to completion; zero nodes remained with in-degree > 0 after queue exhaustion, confirming DAG structure
critical_path_analysis.representative_path_selection_rationale: WP2 (Neuro-Symbolic Planning and Reasoning Engine) selected as the source node for the representative critical path because it produces the planning engine — the foundational computational capability on which all three demonstrator WPs depend for agent-based planning. WP5 selected as the representative demonstrator-layer node. All nine tied paths are of equal structural length (3 edges); none is algorithmically dominant over others.
critical_path_analysis.traceability_note: All nodes in critical_path_nodes (WP2, WP5, WP8, WP9) are confirmed present in dependency_map.nodes. All edges traversed in the critical path (WP2→WP5, WP5→WP8, WP8→WP9) are confirmed present in dependency_map.edges. Critical path is fully traceable to the dependency map per constitutional constraint 2.
